title: brand-atoms v1 shell brand extension
description: Extends a general brand atom with shell-specific bindings for use with aish. Covers prompt segments, separator characters, ANSI escape sequence role bindings, and terminal color support declarations.

id.description: Unique slug for this shell brand extension. Matches the filename without .yaml.

name.description: Human-readable display name for this shell brand.

version.description: SemVer version string for this shell brand atom.

description.description: Optional prose description of the shell brand and its intended use.

base_brand.description: Reference to a brand-atoms brand ID that this shell extension derives from. Does not require the base brand to exist in brands/ (supports external/community themes).

prompt_symbol.description: Terminal prompt symbol displayed at the shell prompt. Examples: '❯', '➜', '$', '▶', '%'.

separator_char.description: Powerline or segment separator character. Use empty string for themes without segment separators.

ansi_256_support.description: Whether this shell brand supports 256-color ANSI palettes (xterm-256color).

truecolor_support.description: Whether this shell brand supports 24-bit true-color terminals.

role_bindings.description: Maps semantic roles to #RRGGBB hex color values for use as ANSI escape sequences or terminal color references.

primary.description: Primary UI color — used for active prompt segments, directory names.

accent.description: Accent color — used for git branch indicators, highlights.

error.description: Error / failure color — used for non-zero exit codes, error messages.

warning.description: Warning color — used for dirty git state, stash indicators.

success.description: Success / clean color — used for zero exit codes, clean git state.

muted.description: Muted / secondary color — used for timestamps, secondary path segments.

tags.description: Taxonomy tags for discovery and filtering.

license.description: SPDX license identifier for the color scheme. Examples: MIT, Apache-2.0, CC0-1.0.
